channel.title: Devblogs.microsoft.com on .NET Ramblings
channel.description: Recent content in Devblogs.microsoft.com on .NET Ramblings

item.title: PowerToys 0.100 is here: new Shortcut Guide, Command Palette improvements and much more!
item.description: Link to article: https://devblogs.microsoft.com/commandline/powertoys-0-100-is-here-new-shortcut-guide-command-palette-improvements-and-much-more

item.title: .NET 11 Preview 5 is now available!
item.description: Today, we are excited to announce the fifth preview release of .NET 11! This release includes improvements across the .NET Runtime, SDK, libraries, ASP.NET Core, .NET MAUI, C#, Entity Framework Core, and more. Check out the linked release notes below and get started today. [cta-button align="center" text="Download .NET 11 Preview 5" url="ht Link to article: https://devblogs.microsoft.com/dotnet/dotnet-11-preview-5

item.title: .NET and .NET Framework June 2026 servicing releases updates
item.description: Welcome to our combined .NET servicing updates for June 2026. Let's get into the latest release of .NET & .NET Framework, here is a quick overview of what's new in our servicing releases: Security improvements .NET and has been refreshed with the latest update as of June 9, 2026. This update contains security and non-security fi Link to article: https://devblogs.microsoft.com/dotnet/dotnet-and-dotnet-framework-june-2026-servicing-updates

item.title: Distributed multi-agent systems with Aspire and Microsoft Agent Framework
item.description: Agentic applications stop being simple very quickly. A useful assistant rarely stays as one model call. It needs tools, live data, specialized agents, orchestration, memory, voice or chat entry points, deployment infrastructure, health checks, and observability. That is the story behind the Link to article: https://devblogs.microsoft.com/aspire/building-distributed-multi-agent-systems-with-aspire-and-microsoft-agent-framework

item.title: ICYMI: Inside the Microsoft Agent Framework: How we designed a layered SDK
item.description: In case you missed it, the Command Line blog was launched last week and has a great article (by yours truly) about our SDK design philosophy with Microsoft Agent Framework. Check it out: Inside the Link to article: https://devblogs.microsoft.com/agent-framework/icymi-inside-the-microsoft-agent-framework-how-we-designed-a-layered-sdk

item.title: Staging Part 2 - Disk Space Optimization
item.description: "Disk space optimizations" is one of the key features of MSIX: Disk space optimizations. With MSIX there is no duplication of files across apps and Windows manages the shared files across apps. The apps are still independent Link to article: https://devblogs.microsoft.com/insidemsix/staging-disk-space-optimization

item.title: Copilot Code Reviews for Azure Repos
item.description: Over the last several years, we have encouraged customers to move their repositories from Azure Repos to GitHub to take advantage of the latest AI powered and agentic development experiences that GitHub is delivering. At the same time, we recognize that migrating repositories to GitHub can vary significantly in complexity depending Link to article: https://devblogs.microsoft.com/devops/copilot-code-reviews-for-azure-repos

item.title: The Microsoft Company Party where everybody played name tag swap
item.description: I learned from a long-retired Microsoft employee about a Company Party that took place around 1984 or so. The company was small enough that a single party could fit the entire company, but not so small that everybody knew everybody else, so each guest was issued a name tag. During the evening, an unofficial game arose in which peopl Link to article: https://devblogs.microsoft.com/oldnewthing/20260609-00/?p=112409

item.title: 🚀React Native Windows v0.83 is here!!
item.description: We’re excited to release React Native Windows 0.83.0, aligned with React Native 0.83.4. React Native Windows v0.83 advances the Fabric Architecture with broader Fabric parity, richer input and automation surfaces, stronger accessibility for hosted XAML islands, and new tooling to measure and protect component performance. Link to article: https://devblogs.microsoft.com/react-native/%f0%9f%9a%80react-native-windows-v0-83-is-here

item.title: Microsoft Build 2026 recap: vision, launches, and top sessions
item.description: Microsoft Build 2026 is a wrap, and there was a LOT. Whether you were there in person, streaming live, or catching up now, this recap covers the biggest announcements, top sessions, and everything you need to keep building. 🎯 Vision lead-off: the system around AI is the differentiator Jay Parikh's post, Link to article: https://devblogs.microsoft.com/blog/build-recap

item.title: .NET at Microsoft Build 2026: Must watch sessions
item.description: That's a wrap on Microsoft Build 2026! From union types in C# to agentic web apps and AI on the edge with .NET MAUI, this year's event showed how .NET 11 is built for the AI era. Whether you joined live or are catching up on demand, here are the .NET sessions worth your time. [alert type="tip" heading="Tip"]Want to binge the whole lineup? W Link to article: https://devblogs.microsoft.com/dotnet/dotnet-at-microsoft-build-2026

item.title: Enterprise Live Migrations: Moving from Azure DevOps Repo to GitHub with minimal disruption
item.description: Over the last several years, we’ve encouraged customers to move their repositories from Azure Repos to GitHub to take advantage of the latest AI-powered and agentic development experiences. For many enterprise teams, however, migrating at scale comes with real constraints. Traditional approaches can require extended downtime - som Link to article: https://devblogs.microsoft.com/devops/enterprise-live-migrations

item.title: The back cover of C++: The Programming Language also raises questions not answered by the front cover
item.description: A little while ago, we considered how the cover of the book C++: The Programming Language raises questions not answered by the cover, since the cover illustratio Link to article: https://devblogs.microsoft.com/oldnewthing/20260605-01/?p=112391

item.title: Rotation revisited: Avoiding having to calculate the gcd when doing cycle decomposition
item.description: Last time, we looked at how clang's libcxx implementation of std::rotate uses cycle decomposition to minimize the number of swaps. Doing so requires calculating the greatest co Link to article: https://devblogs.microsoft.com/oldnewthing/20260605-00/?p=112389

item.title: Frameworks only matter when they force decisions
item.description: Frameworks mean nothing, Link to article: https://devblogs.microsoft.com/all-things-azure/frameworks-only-matter-when-they-force-decisions

item.title: Aspire Multi-repo Rollout at Scale with Agentic AI
item.description: In our previous post, Aspire Multi-Repo Microservices - Windows 365 Integration Journey, we explored how we extended Aspire to unlock multi-repo microservice development, enabling teams to independently build, test, and debug microservices across repositories w Link to article: https://devblogs.microsoft.com/aspire/aspire-windows-365-part2

item.title: Accelerate Edge AI Development with Foundry Local
item.description: Why edge AI development is still hard AI is no longer confined to cloud experiments. Developers are increasingly expected to deliver AI inside apps, devices, and edge systems where responsiveness, privacy, resilience, and local control ar Link to article: https://devblogs.microsoft.com/foundry/accelerate-edge-ai-development-with-foundry-local

item.title: How we Decide Between Keyword and Hybrid Search: 5 Enterprise Evaluation Criteria
item.description: Introduction In a recent engagement, we worked with a customer who already had a similarity search system backed by LanceDB. The foundation was solid: vector search over embedded documents powering retrieval for end user similarity searches. But their use case wasn’t purely semantic. Users frequently searched by identifier nu Link to article: https://devblogs.microsoft.com/ise/keyword-vs-hybrid-search

item.title: Build 2026: From observability to ROI for AI agents on any frameworkÂ
item.description: 9 min read Âˇ June 3, 2026 Âˇ Sebastian KohlmeierÂ Â Shipping an AI agent is the easy part. Keeping itÂ accurate, safe, and accountable in production i Link to article: https://devblogs.microsoft.com/foundry/build-2026-from-observability-to-roi-for-ai-agents-on-any-framework

item.title: Go 1.26.4-1 and 1.25.11-1 Microsoft builds now available
item.description: A new release of the Microsoft build of Go including security fixes is now available for download. For more information about this release and the changes included, see the table below: Microsoft Release Upstream Tag Link to article: https://devblogs.microsoft.com/go/go-1-26-4-1-and-1-25-11-1-microsoft-builds-now-available

item.title: How Microsoft is migrating repositories to GitHub
item.description: For the past decade, Azure DevOps has powered software development at Microsoft, supporting some of our largest repositories and most complex engineering workflows across Azure Repos, Boards, and Pipelines. Software development is being reshaped by AI, and where code lives now have a direct impact on how much value organizations can Link to article: https://devblogs.microsoft.com/devops/how-microsoft-is-migrating-repositories-to-github

item.title: Expanding the Reach of Document Translation - New Capabilities Announced at Microsoft Build
item.description: Our commitment to advancing cross-language communication takes another major step forward at Microsoft Build. Azure Translator in Foundry Tools is expanding Document Translation with a set of new capabilities designed to meet enterprises where their content lives today — across formats, modalities, and languages. From large language model– Link to article: https://devblogs.microsoft.com/foundry/document-translation-build-2026

item.title: Visual Studio Code 1.123
item.description: Learn what's new in Visual Studio Code 1.123 Read the full article Link to article: https://devblogs.microsoft.com/vscode-blog/visual-studio-code-1.123

item.title: Microsoft Agent Framework at BUILD 2026: Agent Harness, Hosted Agents, CodeAct, and more
item.description: Microsoft Agent Framework at BUILD 2026: Agent Harness, Hosted Agents, CodeAct, and more Link to article: https://devblogs.microsoft.com/agent-framework/microsoft-agent-framework-at-build-2026-announce

item.title: Announcing Foundry Managed Compute: Run open models in Microsoft Foundry
item.description: Announcing Foundry Managed Compute: Run open models in Microsoft Foundry Today we're announcing Managed Compute in Microsoft Foundry, a new managed platform for customizing and serving open-source AI models on elastic GPU capacity without the operational burden of running virtual machines, Kubernetes clusters, or mod Link to article: https://devblogs.microsoft.com/foundry/announcing-foundry-managed-compute

item.title: Introducing Agent Optimizer in Foundry Agent Service
item.description: With hosted agents, we made it straightforward to build and deploy agents on Foundry. You write your logic, run azd deploy, and your agent is live. But “live” and “production- Link to article: https://devblogs.microsoft.com/foundry/agent-optimizer-build2026

item.title: Making agent memory more reliable, transparent, and production-ready
item.description: Memory has always mattered for personalization and continuity. But as customers move agents from demos into production, another requirement becomes just as important: reliability.Â Â Enterprise teams need agents that not only rememberÂ facts, butÂ Link to article: https://devblogs.microsoft.com/foundry/memory-build2026

item.title: Discovery to Execution: Scaling Agents with Toolboxes and Routines in Microsoft Foundry
item.description: Tooling doesn’t break at a small scale—it breaks when teams move to production. AI adoption accelerates, so does the number of tools available to them. Discovering, managing and securing the right tools has quickly become one of the biggest challenges in building production-grade agents. Today, we are announcing new capabilities in Toolbox Link to article: https://devblogs.microsoft.com/foundry/toolbox-build-26
